entry.title: Taipei Times: 13 Jul, 2022
entry.content:

  • Japan mourns as funeral for Abe held
  • Air-raid drills to be held across four days: ministry
  • MAC slams Xi’s call for youth to back unification

  • Pond peregrination

entry.title: Washington Post
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Putin to visit Tehran; Russia, Ukraine to discuss Black Sea ‘grain corridor’
  • The euro is nearly equal to the dollar. Here’s why it matters.
  • Mexico’s president meets Biden amid tension over migration, fentanyl
  • Macron defends his support for Uber after leak of company files
  • Brother of disgraced Sri Lankan president blocked from fleeing country
  • Next U.K. prime minister? 8 candidates jockey to replace Boris Johnson.
  • Move faster to help global food crisis, senators urge
  • Russia holding 400 passenger jets hostage in global sanctions fight
  • Key Islamic State figure killed in Syria drone strike, U.S. says
  • How the next U.K. prime minister will be chosen
  • How Abe and Japan became vital to Moon’s Unification Church
  • What to know about the upheaval in Sri Lanka
  • U.K.’s elite Cambridge University opens inquiry into student deaths
  • Photos: Japan bids farewell to Shinzo Abe
  • Japan bids farewell to Shinzo Abe at slain former leader’s funeral
  • U.S. to open new embassies, boost aid in Pacific as China’s sway grows
  • Recording reveals life in captivity for American held by Russian group
  • Fake cricket league hired farmers as pros, duped Russian gamblers, police say
  • Within the war between Russia and Ukraine, a war between Chechens
  • Uber Files put Macron’s neoliberalism in the spotlight
  • Panamanians angry over inflation press on with protests
  • Ukraine reports striking Russian ammunition depot in south
  • UN limits aid to Syrian rebels to 6 months in a Russian win
  • Rights groups: Egypt should open civic space ahead of COP27
  • Ivory Coast calls on Mali to immediately release 49 soldiers
  • New video shows police in Uvalde waiting in hallway while gunman fires
  • In Highland Park, mother who died saving her toddler is honored
  • Senate confirms ATF director nominee Steve Dettelbach
  • Attorney accused of murdering wife and son to be charged, brother says
  • Pennsylvania GOP aims to bypass governor’s veto on abortion restrictions
  • Selfie-taking American rescued after falling into Vesuvius crater
  • This wild ‘I Voted’ sticker could boost turnout, N.Y. county says
  • Sea lions send beach crowds fleeing ‘like Godzilla is chasing them’
  • A death row inmate wants to donate a kidney. Texas won’t let him.
  • A new pilot was forced to make a risky landing. Video captured his feat.
  • It’s been a hard time for librarians. Enter an epic sleepover of stuffed toys.
  • Yosemite wildfire doubles in size, threatens over 500 giant sequoias
  • Jill Biden causes minor flap by comparing Latino diversity to tacos
  • Jan. 6 hearing: Trump tried to contact committee witness, an effort referred to Justice Department, Cheney says
  • Post Politics Now: ‘It matters, but it’s not enough,’ Biden says of new gun law
  • Jan. 6 hearing expected to focus on link between militants, White House
  • With Fed rate increases, the economy’s free ride is over
  • Elon Musk says Trump should ‘hang up his hat and sail into the sunset’
  • Euro and dollar near parity as recession fears grow in Europe
  • At Invitation Homes, unpermitted work leaves leaky plumbing, faulty repairs, renters say
  • Twitter sues Elon Musk, setting stage for epic legal battle
  • Twitter went easy on Trump because it ‘relished’ the power, ex-employee says
  • Is Amazon Prime worth it for you?
  • Elevator condo buildings at transit-oriented community in Ashburn, Va.
  • You can build your own wood fence and get fancy with it
  • First residents move into former Fannie Mae headquarters
  • What the World Got Wrong About Shinzo Abe
  • Fed Is Not Falling Into an Emerging Markets Trap. Not Even Close
  • Housing Inventories May Not Save Prices After All
  • Why the Tories Are Britain’s Party of Diversity
  • Why Croatia Sees Joining the Euro as Path to Security
  • How Europe Became So Dependent on Putin for Its Gas
  • Why the Euro Has Tumbled Near Parity to the US Dollar
  • How UK’s Tories Will Elect Leader to Replace Johnson
  • Can you spare a minute to look 13 billion years back in time?
  • Trump, and Trump alone, turned Jan. 6 into a debacle for our democracy
  • The Jan. 6 hearing showed how lies can kill
  • Is the Democrats’ problem Biden or inflation?
  • Will Lindsey Graham explain his 2020 call to Brad Raffensperger?
  • On eve of Biden visit, a court case shows the limits of Saudi tolerance
  • The years and billions spent on the James Webb telescope? Worth it.
  • Congress should take up the gauntlet thrown down by Supreme Court
  • Brittney Griner’s fate poses a warning to U.S. sports and Hollywood
  • If pols can’t make inflation better, they should at least stop making it worse
  • Republicans won’t say what they’re for. But it’s not hard to figure out.
  • The Biden administration’s bold embrace of harm reduction will save lives
  • Vanita Gupta on the future of policing and American democracy
  • As dangerous algal blooms grow, agencies need to pick up the pace
  • Uber’s story shows what happens when a firm moves fast and breaks things
  • 5 things we learned from the Jan. 6 committee’s latest hearing
  • New York Times won’t correct its errant essay on ectopic pregnancy
  • Dramatic moments from deprogrammed right-wingers indict the whole GOP
  • Twitter is fixable. But pity the fool who does the next deal with Elon Musk.
  • Conservatives hope to turn back the cultural clock. Can they succeed?
  • Biden’s scheme to cap oil prices reflects his incoherent Russia policy
  • The evidence leads to Trump
  • Cartoon by Walt Handelsman
  • Tracking sexual violence in the war in Ukraine is easier said than done
  • Canada’s internet outage should encourage us to dismantle our telecom oligopoly
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: NY Times
entry.content:

entry.title: Financial Times
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

entry.content:

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 12 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 12 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-12 22:55PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • US should expand Hong Kong sanctions to mid-tier officials, panel is told
  • Digital Red Packets and the Power of Sharing Online
  • Can Robots Be Used to Obtain Honest Customer Feedback?
  • How Marketers Can Leverage on the Power of Comparison
  • Sitting on a Gold Mine: Do People Spend More When Home Prices Rise?
  • Meeting Market Appetites with a Distinctive Tam Jai Taste

  • Hong Kong public transport firms Citybus and New World First Bus to join forces in bid to stem losses

  • Hong Kong veteran activist Koo Sze-yiu sentenced to 9 months in jail for planning to display coffin bearing slogans outside Beijing’s liaison office

  • What you need to know about Hong Kong’s new health code system to identify coronavirus cases
  • Hong Kong’s nano flats: does falling demand point to the end of container-size abodes?

  • Newest Chinese aircraft carrier Fujian gives PLA an edge in Taiwan Strait: analyst
  • US can take lessons from Australia in reducing trade reliance on China, US lawmaker says
  • China to buy Xinjiang cotton, but state stockpiling won’t save mills from massive losses amid US ban
  • ‘Magnetic shields’ could help bacteria survive on Mars, Chinese scientists say
  • Russian telecoms giant MegaFon eyes Chinese 5G equipment after Western suppliers pull out amid war in Ukraine
  • China’s Tsinghua Unigroup completes debt restructuring, ownership change to keep afloat its major semiconductor operations
  • Jose Eduardo dos Santos: the Angolan leader who looked to China to fund reconstruction
  • Beijing protests after Taiwanese vice-president attends Shinzo Abe’s funeral
  • Taiwan to hold island-wide air raid drills simulating PLA attacks

  • China ‘not to blame’ for African debt crisis, it’s the West: study
  • Should Hong Kong give the red light to mainland-style coronavirus health codes?
  • No summer treat for China’s young gamers: Tencent says three hours a week game time limit remains
  • China’s banking-crisis victims promised money, but outcry as ‘it doesn’t solve underlying problem’
  • ‘Look with 2 eyes’: Beijing envoy urges South Korean media to report on ‘real China’
  • Coronavirus: new variants raise questions about sustainability of China’s zero-Covid policy
  • China debt: local governments target amateur investors in new round of risky credit expansion
  • Largest Chinese patrol ship in Taiwan Strait opens up diplomatic route to law enforcement: analysts
  • Sri Lanka President Gotabaya Rajapaksa flees for the Maldives as country struggles with economic crisis
  • Durian fever: China’s Wang Yi raves over Malaysian Musang King cheesecake
  • Thailand close to approving chemical castration for sex offenders
  • Where is Sri Lanka’s President Gotabaya Rajapaksa? And why are protesters in his pool, in his bed, and playing his piano?
  • Marcos urged to stick to South China Sea ruling amid Philippines’ ‘sweet spot’ in US-China diplomacy
  • Shinzo Abe’s killing spotlights Unification Church’s ties with Japanese conservatives
  • Japan bids farewell to Shinzo Abe with funeral procession through heart of Tokyo
  • How Shinzo Abe’s push for reform at home and abroad made Japan stronger and safer
  • Airport staff block Sri Lanka’s president from fleeing crisis-hit country
  • Miss Universe Bhutan blazes LGBT trail as only openly gay celebrity in a land where same-sex activity was long illegal
  • Australia urges US to expand Asia military presence or face ‘catastrophic’ balance of power failure
  • Asia needs leaders of the calibre of Singapore’s Lee Kuan Yew to navigate a messy world
  • US launches Asia charm offensive to counter China’s diplomacy after Blinken-Wang Yi meet
  • South Korea’s Yonsei students under fire for suing underpaid cleaners over ‘noisy’ protests
  • Twitter sues Elon Musk to make him complete US$44 billion deal
  • Donald Trump supporters attacked US Capitol after seeing his tweet as ‘call to arms’, investigators say
  • Islamic State Syria chief killed in US drone strike, Pentagon says
  • Emmys 2022: Succession tops nominations as Squid Game makes history
  • Field narrows to replace UK PM Boris Johnson as nominations close: and then there were 8
  • Nasa’s James Webb Space Telescope shows where stars are born and how they die
  • Britons accuse UAE of torture at UN committee in rare allegations against the Gulf State
  • WHO says Covid-19 still global health emergency as cases up 30 per cent in fortnight
  • UK special forces killed Afghan detainees in suspicious circumstances, probe finds
  • Ukraine’s military starts counter-attack in occupied Kherson
  • China’s Ganfeng to pay nearly US$1 billion for its third lithium mine in Argentina to bolster resource for electric vehicle batteries
  • Elon Musk says it’s time for Donald Trump to ‘sail into the sunset’
  • Iran providing Russia ‘weapons-capable’ drones for war in Ukraine, US says
  • Joe Biden seeks to persuade Saudi Arabia to pump more oil as prices soar
  • Italy’s watchdog alleges TikTok breached EU privacy rules, warning the short video platform about targeted ads
  • Non-white patients get less ICU oxygen because of flawed device: study
  • First image from Nasa’s James Webb Space Telescope offers deepest-ever look at cosmos
  • Can G7’s infrastructure plan really challenge China’s Belt and Road Initiative?
  • Why US economic strength and China commodity demand bode ill for recession bets
  • Hong Kong to benefit as China’s carbon neutral goal and urbanisation drive commodities in post-pandemic era, HKEX CEO says
  • XPeng’s robotics unit raises US$100 million with the aim of commercialising its business in two years
  • Hong Kong blockchain unicorn Animoca Brands raises another US$75 million as crypto crash persists
  • China’s Tianqi Lithium was due for drop, analysts say, as value falls US$4.5 billion ahead of Hong Kong trading debut
  • Hundreds of Hong Kong land sites with room for at least 20,000 new flats stuck in limbo as owners call for government action
  • Temasek sees more balanced risk-reward in China markets after recent setbacks of Alibaba, Didi and other tech bets
  • Covid-19 pandemic hits value of Hong Kong’s subsidised Home Ownership Scheme flats, study shows
  • R&F buys up to four years of breathing room as creditors give go-ahead for developer to group 10 offshore bonds into three amortised notes
  • Hong Kong’s listed firms set for more climate risk reporting, with roll out of new consolidated ISSB standards on the horizon
  • Why Hong Kong’s community malls are crucial to the future of the city’s retail sector
  • Chinese hotpot chain Haidilao mulls spinning off overseas restaurants, separate listing in Hong Kong
  • Alibaba, BYD drag Hong Kong stocks to six-week low as Covid-19 cases, tech fines crimp optimism
  • Twitter hits back at Musk, demands US$44 billion deal be completed as it prepares for lawsuit
  • Viral Kyrgyzstan avalanche video another warning of climate breakdown we’ll probably ignore
  • Hair removal: all you need to know about sugar, wax, cream, machines and laser beams
  • 4 hot new chefs at Shanghai restaurants, from 3-Michelin-star Taian Table to YongFoo Elite and Urban Cafe
  • Sushi without wasabi? The condiment’s future is at risk because of climate change, Japanese farmers fear
  • Chilli Laugh Story movie review: Mirror’s Edan Lui leads Lunar New Year comedy that mixes family antics, naughty jokes and social commentary
  • James Bond movies were filmed there – now it’s being turned into Raffles London luxury hotel
  • Kyoto wants Japan travel to open quicker and foreign tourists to return, though not everyone yearns to see the masses again
  • Change the food court, change the world: eco-innovator Matt Reid on sustainability and his new dining concept in Hong Kong
  • When an American illegally entered China by boat – he did it ‘out of curiosity’
  • Hong Kong’s water supplies built by the British allowed the city to grow and thrive more than any other factor
  • Want to lose weight, but always hungry? Research suggests a way to help obese people feel full while eating less
  • Meet Elena Rybakina, the calm 23-year-old who won Wimbledon 2022: the Russian-born Kazakhstani tennis champ is known for her colourful Adidas outfits … and adorable animal Instagram snaps
  • Elon Musk vs Twitter: what happens next? The Tesla billionaire ditched his US$44 billion deal, the social media platform sued … and there are 3 possible endings, according to experts
  • Is Awkwafina … uncancelled? Inside the star’s epic comeback after her ‘blaccent’ backlash, from new films with Sandra Oh and Nicolas Cage, to attending the Met Gala in Gucci
  • Meet Alexandre Arnault, the hunky son of LVMH billionaire Bernard Arnault, Europe’s richest man: he’s worked with Beyoncé and Blackpink’s Rosé, and Kanye West performed at his wedding
  • She lied! RHOSLC’s Jen Shah set for prison after guilty plea: The Real Housewives of Salt Lake City star denied fraud claims at first, but now the telemarketing scam could cost her US$9.5 million
  • Who was Oprah Winfrey’s dad, Vernon Winfrey? The former barber, who passed away peacefully at 89, was the billionaire media mogul’s inspiration – and didn’t care if he was her biological dad or not
  • Déjà vu? Kate Middleton and George channel Diana and William at Wimbledon: the young prince’s tennis debut looked almost identical to his dad’s appearance alongside his own mum 31 years earlier
  • 10 richest God of Gamblers cast members – net worths, ranked: Hong Kong icons Andy Lau, Stephen Chow, Donnie Yen, Tony Leung and Chow Yun-fat make millions – but who’s the mystery billionaire on top?
  • Paris Haute Couture Week: Elie Saab’s gender-bending autumn/winter 2022-23 show featured men sweeping down the runway in red carpet-ready gowns and capes
  • Big fat Ambani wedding: Mukesh hosted a glam garden extravaganza for niece Isheta Salgaocar at his US$73 million Stoke Park estate, with family including Anant, Isha and Shloka Mehta in attendance
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: ChannelNewsAsia: 12 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Canada's Rogers to credit customers with equivalent of five days service
  • Sri Lanka President Gotabaya Rajapaksa flees to Maldives ahead of expected resignation

  • TikTok delays changes to privacy policy over Europe data concerns

  • New CNB director to be appointed on Nov 6
  • Grab refining controls so drivers can only mark arrival when at pick-up point or 'very close' to it
  • Live performances, activity booths to resume at Istana open house for National Day celebration

  • Temasek Holdings net portfolio value crosses S$400 billion for the first time

entry.title: Guardian: 12 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • January 6 hearings: Trump tried to contact witness, Cheney says – live

  • US law will overrule states for abortions in cases of medical emergencies

  • Charcuterie’s link to colon cancer confirmed by French authorities
  • Uber stormed through an open door – now politicians must change the locks | Jamie Susskind
  • Putin due in Tehran as US says Iran to supply drones to Russia
  • Elon Musk wants everyone to have big families like him – but who’s going to pay for them? | Arwa Mahdawi
  • Getty opens access to 30,000 images of black diaspora in UK and US
  • Military police ask BBC for details ahead of Panorama’s SAS death squad claims
  • Heathrow asks airlines to stop selling summer flights as it caps passengers
  • ‘They were taking us for a ride’: how Uber used investor cash to seduce drivers
  • The Tory green consensus is breaking – this leadership contest could spell the end of net zero | Helena Horton
  • Democrats are certain to lose seats in the midterms. But how many – and why? | Musa al-Gharbi
  • Euro nears parity with dollar as pound hits two-year low
  • Uber used Greyball fake app to evade police across Europe, leak reveals
  • Breaking from China’s clean energy dominance ‘imperative’, US and Australia say after new climate tech deal
  • Nearly $2tn of damage inflicted on other countries by US emissions
  • Shinzo Abe is gone, but his controversial vision for Japan lives on | Jeff Kingston
  • Chasiv Yar death toll rises to 34 in one of Russia’s deadliest attacks so far
  • Uber paid academics six-figure sums for research to feed to the media
Latest international news, sport and comment from the Guardian

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 13 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.title: NY Times (Print): 12 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia Steps Up Attacks on Civilian Areas, Even With Advance Paused

  • Joe Biden Is Too Old to Be President Again

  • A Year After Mass Protest, Cubans Face Stark Choice: ‘Prison or Exile’
